title: viewer: button clicks silently no-op when no wait-event consumer is attached
description:
**Headline UX failure** from python419/anthony's verification run (msg hq-wisp-pp0lbh, 2026-05-17). Real users clicking buttons in the viewer get no visible feedback when no Claude Code consumer is running `review-pdf wait-event`. AJB clicked Skip / Next / Surface; HTTP 204 returned for each; state-events.jsonl recorded the events; state.json never changed; current_annotation_id never advanced. From the user's perspective: 'buttons don't work'.

Root cause (by design, per spec §10.5): /api/events POST appends to state-events.jsonl and returns 204. State mutation requires an external consumer (Claude running wait-event loop) to dispatch CLI commands. With the embedded terminal (rev-dyn) lowering the bar to opening the viewer standalone, this assumption now breaks routinely.

Three proposed fix shapes (recommend doing 1 + 2; 3 is a bigger architectural call):

1. **Auto-dispatch status-neutral actions server-side.** Prev / Next / set-current have no state-machine implications. Server has state.json + the navigate intent — it can call apply.set_current_annotation directly without violating the engine-is-sole-writer principle (server IS calling the engine, just inline). Closes the silence for the most common nav case.

2. **Surface 'no consumer attached' to the user.** When a status-mutating action (approve/reject/redraft/skip/surface) sits unprocessed for ~2s, show a toast or status-line update: 'No active consumer. Your click was logged but not processed yet.' Cheap; prevents user confusion.

3. **Optional: built-in lightweight consumer in the server.** Major architectural shift — moving engine semantics inside the server. Open question whether this is worth it. The clean event-source model has value.

This is rev-bus's other half: it added Prev/Next buttons + set-current CLI, but didn't close the loop between button click and CLI call.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback-run-2.md (forthcoming)
close_reason: Closed

title: viewer: _render_frame omits required template context (BLOCKER)
description:
Phase 0 viewer is unreachable. `_render_frame` at `src/review_pdf_to_latex/server.py:271` passes only `current_state` and `mode` to `templates/frame.html`, but the template requires at minimum: `diff2html_present`, `project_root`, `phase`, `order`, `annotation_index`, `total_annotations`, `current_annotation` (plus nested fields). The Jinja env at `server.py:49` is configured with `jinja2.StrictUndefined`, so the first missing variable raises `UndefinedError` mid-render and the response body is never written.

Symptom: browser shows 'failed to open page'; curl returns empty reply.

First failing reference: `{% if diff2html_present %}` at templates/frame.html:215.

Impact:
- Phase 0 manual-mapping UI unreachable (9 needs_review entries cannot be resolved in the browser).
- Phase 2a Ratify entirely unreachable.
- Both `--mapping-mode` and normal mode share the broken render path.

Suggested fix shape (see handoff for full snippet): build a full context dict in `_render_frame` derived from state.json (current_annotation, annotation_index, total_annotations, phase, order, project_root, diff2html_present).

Also: add a smoke test (`GET /` returns 200 against a fixture project). The synthetic test suite never exercised the rendered HTML, which is how this slipped through.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BLOCKER #1)
close_reason: Fixed in 5f139e1: _render_frame now assembles the full context (current_state, mode, project_root, phase, order, annotation_index, total_annotations, current_annotation, diff2html_present, plus the 3-pane fields for normal mode and needs_review_annotations/tex_files for mapping mode). Added two real-render smoke tests in test_server.py covering both modes. Verified end-to-end: extract → serve → GET / returns 200 with rendered HTML, in both normal and --mapping-mode.

title: [file-tree] search filter scope limited to currently-rendered (expanded) folders; should search full tree
description: User feedback 2026-05-23: typing into 'Filter by name…' only matches files inside already-expanded folders, because applyFilterToDom (tree.ts:260) walks the DOM, and folders are lazy-loaded (per the file's header comment). Files in collapsed folders are invisible to the filter — looks like 'filter isn't working' from the user's POV. Fix: filter must operate on the underlying tree model (FileTree's data) and expand ancestors of matches, not on the rendered DOM. Touch: desktop/renderer/tree.ts setFilter + applyFilterToDom + the lazy-load loader.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via live test 2026-05-23:

Two distinct bugs found in the same code path, splitting for clarity:

BUG A (this bead, P1 — trivial fix): tree.ts:297 sets r.hidden = true on non-matches, which SHOULD hide via UA stylesheet '[hidden] { display: none }'. But styles.css:1027 declares '.tree-row { display: flex; ... }' which overrides the UA rule (same specificity, later in cascade). Result: hidden attribute set but element still renders.

Fix: add to styles.css after line 1037:
  .tree-row[hidden] { display: none; }

BUG B (split to new bead rev-???): collapsed folders aren't searched because applyFilterToDom walks the rendered DOM, and folders are lazy-loaded. Needs filter to operate on the data model.

User-observed behavior 2026-05-23: 'if the folder is closed - it does not surface the file. if the folder is open it highlights it but does not filter the view in any way' — exactly matches both bugs above.
close_reason: Closed

title: [agent-pane] phone/t3 session chips don't switch backend session — purely cosmetic
description: User test 2026-05-23: clicked between 'phone' and 't3' chips while running a session-unique marker prompt. Same marker returned in both; status footer session ID stayed at c3bf24ab across toggles. Conclusion: the chips relabel but do not route to different backend sessions. Probably expected pending M-int-4c worker routing, but the chips appearing functional is misleading — users will think they switched and then act on stale assumptions. Options: (a) wire to actual session switch (M-int-4c work), (b) disable chips with tooltip 'multi-session worker routing — coming in M-int-4c', or (c) hide chips entirely until backend is real. title: viewer: embedded Claude Code terminal pane (xterm.js + pty bridge)
description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-q7p
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T17:24:56Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: review_pdf_to_latex/crew/anthony
convoy_id: hq-cv-yni4o
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Add a side/bottom pane in the existing HTML viewer that hosts an interactive Claude Code terminal so the reviewer can have surface-intent conversations (Phase 2b) without alt-tabbing between viewer and terminal.

# Decision: HTML + xterm.js, NOT Electron

Embedding direction: the viewer must be host-shell-agnostic (browser tab, Gas Town webview, future Gas City auth-gated page). HTML+localhost composes; Electron apps don't compose into other shells. xterm.js works identically in every embedding mode.

# Architecture sketch

- **Frontend**: xterm.js + xterm-addon-fit dropped into templates/static/. A new pane in frame.html (or a collapsible bottom drawer) hosts the terminal. CSS already supports dark theme — xterm.js inherits via theme config.
- **Backend**: WebSocket endpoint on the Python server bridging to a pty running `claude` (or whichever command the user configures). stdlib `http.server` doesn't speak WebSocket — three paths:
  - A. Add `websockets` (asyncio) library, run on a separate thread alongside http.server. Cleanest.
  - B. SSE for server→client + POST for client→server. No WS dep, but two channels.
  - C. `wsproto` (low-level WS protocol) wired into the existing http.server. No asyncio.
  Prefer A — well-trodden, easy to test, websockets is ~150KB.
- **Pty**: stdlib `pty` + `os.fork`/`subprocess`. Already cross-platform on macOS + Linux.
- **Spawn target**: configurable via `.review-config.toml` `terminal_command` key (default: `claude`). Allows users to spawn whatever shell/agent they want.

# Open questions

1. **Does the embedded terminal SHARE the parent Claude Code session, or spawn a fresh one?**
   - Sharing requires pty re-attach (hard, racy, platform-specific).
   - Fresh spawn means two parallel Claude sessions — the reviewer can chat with one while the orchestrating session does other work. Probably what we want.
2. **Auth / multi-user**: out of scope for v1 (localhost only). When this lands in Gas City, the bridge endpoint needs an auth gate. Filed as a follow-up risk; not blocking.
3. **Layout**: side pane (4-column) or bottom drawer (toggleable)? Drawer probably wins — preserves the existing 3-pane width for the annotation review.

# Acceptance

- frame.html has a toggleable terminal pane.
- xterm.js + addons vendored under templates/static/.
- Python server exposes `/ws/terminal` (or similar) that bridges to a configurable subprocess via pty.
- Pane works in standalone browser, in iframe, and in a Gas Town webview.
- Reviewer can type a SURFACE conversation in the embedded terminal while looking at the annotation in the main pane.

# Out of scope (file as follow-ups if needed)

- Sharing the parent Claude Code session
- Recording the terminal session into surface_chat_log automatically
- Multiple concurrent terminals
- Auth gate (Gas City prerequisite)

Source: AJB conversation 2026-05-17 — wanted a Claude window in the viewer for in-flow Phase 2b surface conversations. Discussed Electron and decided HTML+xterm.js wins on embeddability into Gas Town and eventually Gas City.
notes:
Implemented per acceptance criteria:
- frame.html: toggleable bottom-drawer terminal pane (id=terminal-drawer), topbar button (id=terminal-toggle). Drawer height driven by CSS custom property; hidden when terminal disabled or xterm assets absent.
- Vendored xterm@5.3.0 (xterm.js, xterm.css) and xterm-addon-fit@0.8.0 under templates/static/.
- /ws/terminal endpoint in server.py upgrades to WebSocket and hands off to terminal.run_bridge. 403 when disabled, 426 when not a valid upgrade.
- pty bridge: stdlib pty.fork + os.execvp, two threads (pty->ws binary frames, ws->pty bytes), TIOCSWINSZ via tunneled JSON {"t":"resize","cols":N,"rows":N} text frames.
- Config: .review-config.toml -> terminal_command (default "claude") and terminal_enabled (default true). Broken/missing config degrades silently.
- HTTPServer -> ThreadingHTTPServer so long-lived WS doesn't block other viewer requests.
- No new Python deps: implemented RFC 6455 framing inline (~150 LOC). Project still has only 4 runtime deps.

Tests: 19 new in tests/test_terminal.py — RFC 6455 §1.3 handshake example, framing parsers/builders (mask enforcement, payload cap, extended length), route surface (403/426), config loader (valid/disable/missing/broken-toml), end-to-end bridge through `cat` validating echo and resize-frame consumption. Full suite: 328 passed, 1 skipped (pre-existing).

Embeddability verified: viewer remains a single localhost HTTP origin -> composes into iframe, Gas Town webview, or future Gas City auth-gated page without changes.

Out of scope (filed in commit message as follow-ups): parent-session re-attach, recording into surface_chat_log, multiple terminals, auth gate (Gas City prereq).

Commits on branch: 1a7cc7a (auto WIP checkpoint by harness, all code) + 24ec7be (CHANGELOG + full feature description).
close_reason: Closed

title: extract: recover highlighted_text from bbox when pdfannots.gettext() returns empty
description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-x50
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T17:26:49Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: review_pdf_to_latex/crew/anthony
convoy_id: hq-cv-pcnii
formula_vars: base_branch=main

When pdfannots fails to extract the underlying text for a Highlight annotation (annotation's quad points don't align with the page's text run — common for Acrobat re-saves or RESTORED PDFs reconstructed from a corrupted source), `highlighted_text` is left empty and the annotation falls into `needs_review` with no anchor text for Phase 1.

In the first real run (COTA Impact Report v2.0), 8 of 9 needs_review entries hit this — the text was in the PDF but pdfannots couldn't link it through the quad points.

Fix: in `read_annotations`, when `raw.gettext()` returns empty for a Highlight, fall back to bbox-region text extraction. Two viable backends:
- A. `pdfplumber` with `page.within_bbox(...).extract_text()`. Adds one dep.
- B. Shell to `pdftotext -bbox-layout` (already implicitly required via Poppler/pdftoppm) and parse the bbox HTML.

Prefer A — Python API, robust, fewer subprocess invocations.

Add an `extraction_method` field to each annotation: `pdfannots | bbox_fallback | failed`. Schema bump if needed.

Does NOT solve true sticky notes (Text annotations with no underlying highlight). Those remain `highlighted_text == ""` and route to manual mapping. Consider a follow-up bead for spatial association (link a free-floating sticky to the nearest highlight).

Companion to rev-mvd (which addresses how the engine handles empty before_text in Phase 1). This bead addresses the upstream cause: we lose the text at extract time.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BUG #5 root cause) + AJB conversation 2026-05-17
close_reason: Fixed (for real) in commit ee03ae2: within_bbox → crop with raw-orientation fallback. Polecat's earlier 'no-changes' close was wrong — the actual within_bbox bug was live. python419/anthony's diagnostic confirmed crop recovers usable text in 7/9 of the COTA RESTORED PDF's needs_review cases. Awaiting verification against AJB's preserved state.

title: engine: needs_review with empty highlighted_text is unresolvable by viewer alone
description:
attached_molecule: [deleted:rev-wisp-u9m]
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T16:43:43Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-mh2ta
formula_vars: base_branch=main

8 of 9 `needs_review` entries in the test run had `highlighted_text == ''` (pdfannots couldn't read the underlying text run; only bbox coordinates were present). Even if the user maps `latex_file` + `line_range` in mapping-mode UI, the engine has no `before_text` for Phase 1 to anchor on.

Root cause: PDFs reconstructed by re-injecting annotations from a corrupted source onto a clean PDF can carry only bbox coordinates, not the underlying text run.

Impact: user must provide BOTH file+line range AND the new text in Phase 1, since there's no `before_text` to compare. Viewer has no surface for 'you'll need to type the intended edit text here too'.

Fix options:
- A. When `highlighted_text == ''`, render a banner in the mapping-mode card explaining the constraint.
- B. In Phase 1, when `before_text` is empty/absent, skip apply-revert-on-failure and emit `surfaced_pending` immediately — defer all empty-before_text annotations to Phase 2b. **Recommended (cleaner long-term answer).**

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BUG #5)
close_reason: implemented: bootstrap_state routes empty-highlighted_text annotations (non-trigger_match) directly to surfaced_pending, bypassing Phase 1 per option B; tests added; full suite passes (338 non-slow + 8 e2e)

title: surface trigger: literal 'claude surface this' misses common reviewer phrasing
description:
Current trigger appears to be the literal substring `claude surface this` (case-insensitive). In a real run, only 3 of ~6 surface-intent annotations matched. Misses: 'Surface this whole table...', 'SURFACE THIS - not sure this is accurate'.

Workflow risk: when mapping resolves needs_review → pending, those annotations enter Phase 1 mechanical batch and the engine attempts an edit — wrong outcome for an annotation the human meant for conversation.

Fix options:
- A. Broaden trigger to `(?i)\\bsurface this\\b` — case-insensitive, drop 'claude' prefix requirement.
- B. Project-configurable: `.review-config.toml` with `surface_trigger = '...'` regex.
- C. Both — ship broadened default, allow override. **Recommended.**

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BUG #4)
close_reason: Fixed in 83f0880: default broadened to 'surface this' (substring, case-insensitive); added .review-config.toml with surface_trigger key; precedence is CLI arg > config > default. Legacy 'claude surface this' comments still match via substring containment. 10 new tests in test_extract.py (parametrized vocabulary cases, config loading, malformed-config degradation, run_extract precedence).

title: cli: --project-dir is top-level only but skill puts it on subcommands
description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-t9y
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T16:41:56Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
convoy_id: hq-cv-vmujw
formula_vars: base_branch=main

argparse declares `--project-dir` only on the top-level parser, but the skill (`~/.claude/skills/review-pdf-to-latex/SKILL.md`) writes it after the subcommand in every example. The CLI rejects it with no hint:

```
$ review-pdf extract --pdf foo.pdf --project-dir /path/to/proj
review-pdf: error: unrecognized arguments: --project-dir /path/to/proj
```

Working invocation:

```
review-pdf --project-dir /path/to/proj extract --pdf foo.pdf
```

Fix options:
- A. Move `--project-dir` into each subcommand's parser (more flexibility).
- B. Keep top-level only and rewrite every skill example (cleaner but breaks muscle memory).
- C. Accept both positions: add `--project-dir` to subcommand parsers, subcommand-level value wins (most forgiving). **Recommended.**

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BUG #2)
notes:
Fix: Option C (accept both positions, sub-level wins).

Changes:
- src/review_pdf_to_latex/cli.py: refactored _add_global_args() to support both top-level and subparser attachment; subparser version uses argparse.SUPPRESS as default so it only overrides namespace when user actually passes the flag. Called _add_global_args(p, on_subparser=True) on all 14 subparsers.
- tests/test_cli.py: added 4 test functions (17 parametrized cases) verifying post-subcommand placement works for all 14 subcommands, sub-level wins when both supplied, pre-subcommand placement still works, and default falls back to cwd.

Result: 'review-pdf extract --pdf foo --project-dir P' now parses; 'review-pdf --project-dir P extract --pdf foo' still works; if both given, subcommand value wins.

All 360 tests pass.
close_reason: Merged to main via refinery (25d63b2)

title: Autonomous sweep: desktop bugs + UI polish
description:

title: M-md-5: Edit-vs-comment interaction polish
description:
Final polish milestone — the small UX nits surfaced by use.

title: M-md-4: Comment anchoring (hybrid scheme)
description:
Comments on .md regions using the hybrid anchoring scheme decided in scoping.

**On comment create:**
- Selection-to-comment flow mirrors PDF reviewer's UX
- Capture { char_start, char_end, prefix: ~40ch, suffix: ~40ch, quoted_text }
- Create CM6 anchor for live-tracking via ChangeSet.mapPos()

**During session:**
- ChangeSet.mapPos() migrates anchors as user edits — comments stay visually attached
- New desktop/shared/md/anchors.ts for fuzzy-snippet types + match logic

**On save:**
- Persisted as md-fuzzy-snippet anchors in the sidecar (alongside pdf-glyph-rect for PDFs)
- char_start/char_end become hints, prefix/suffix/quoted_text are the stable recovery data

**On reopen:**
- Fuzzy-match anchors against current file content using prefix/suffix/quoted_text
- Orphaned anchors (quoted text deleted or heavily edited) surfaced as "lost anchor" in comment list — still readable, no position highlight
acceptance_criteria:
- Create comment on selection → edit text around it → comment stays attached
- Quit and reopen → comment re-anchors via fuzzy match
- Delete the quoted text entirely → comment shows as orphaned (visible in list, no highlight)
- Multiple comments on different regions track independently during edits
- Sidecar contains md-fuzzy-snippet anchors with prefix/suffix/quoted_text
close_reason: Closed

title: M-md-3: Debounced save + modified-state model
description:
Persistence and state machine for .md editing.

- Debounced 500ms-idle write to disk
- documentModifiedById store slice tracks per-file modified state (separate from draftsModifiedById for comments)
- File-tree row indicator: dot/asterisk for source-modified, existing indicator for draft-comments
- Bundle export: snapshot semantics — on-disk source at time of export + sidecar comments at time of export
- External-modification detection: file watcher on open .md files; if disk-mtime advances and we have unsaved changes → modal "File modified externally, reload (lose your changes) or keep (will overwrite on next save)?"
- Save-on-blur as safety net even with debounce in flight
acceptance_criteria:
- Edit, pause, check disk — new contents appear on disk after ~500ms
- Close and reopen — edits persisted
- External rewrite while editor has unsaved changes triggers conflict modal
- File-tree row shows modified indicator distinct from draft-comments indicator
- Bundle export reflects current source + current comments at export time
close_reason: Closed

title: M-md-2: CodeMirror 6 live-preview editor
description:
The core editing UX — Obsidian-style live preview.

- Install CM6 deps: @codemirror/state, @codemirror/view, @codemirror/lang-markdown, @codemirror/commands, @codemirror/language
- CM6 mounted in MarkdownViewer with @codemirror/lang-markdown
- Decorations hide syntax marks (*, _, #, [], (), fence backticks) on lines without the cursor
- Cursor on a line reveals the raw marks for that line only
- Code blocks stay fenced — no decoration removal inside fences

title: M-md-0: FileViewer abstraction + sidecar identity migration
description:
Load-bearing foundation milestone. No user-visible .md behavior yet, but everything downstream depends on this. Also resolves rev-a2f (PDF reopen bug) as a side effect.

**FileViewer abstraction:**
- Define FileViewer interface that PdfViewer and MarkdownViewer both implement
- Refactor middle-pane mount in desktop/renderer/index.ts to pick viewer by classifier output
- Classifier at desktop/main/index.ts:57 already routes .md/.markdown — currently dead-ends

**Sidecar identity migration (sha256 → path-mirroring):**
- New draftsPathFor(docPath) — path-based instead of sha256-based
- One-shot startup migration: walk .review-state/drafts/, for each <sha256>.json look up the matching doc by recomputing hashes, rewrite to <basename>.json with doc_fingerprint populated
- Idempotent — safe to re-run; skip already-migrated sidecars
- doc_fingerprint stored IN the sidecar: { title_from_frontmatter, first_500_chars_sha256, anchor_count, last_known_path }

**Rename-recovery flow:**
1. Open doc at path P → lookup sidecar at P → miss
2. Scan sidecar dir for fingerprint match (first_500_chars_sha256)
3. If match → prompt "Found drafts for this file at <old-path>. Relink?"
4. On confirm: move sidecar, update last_known_path

**Schema changes:**
- DraftsFile in desktop/shared/types.ts gets anchor.kind discriminator (pdf-glyph-rect | md-fuzzy-snippet) + doc_fingerprint field
- desktop/main/index.ts:43-44 draftsPathFor becomes path-based
- New desktop/main/sidecar-migration.ts for startup migration

**Files:**
- New: desktop/shared/file-viewer.ts (interface)
- New: desktop/main/sidecar-migration.ts
- Modify: desktop/main/index.ts (draftsPathFor, classifier routing, migration call)
- Modify: desktop/renderer/index.ts (middle-pane viewer dispatch)
- Extend: desktop/shared/types.ts (DraftsFile schema)

title: M-col-1c: fix global keydown handler for column-mode terminal focus
description:
ChatView.tsx:2467-2555 registers a window-level keydown listener. The terminal-focus check at :2473 uses a DOM-global isTerminalFocused() (terminalFocus.ts:6) that just asks document.activeElement. In columns mode, both ChatView instances mount their own listener; both fire on every keystroke. If col0's terminal is focused, both col0 and col1's handlers think 'a terminal is focused' and may both respond to a shortcut.

title: M-col-1b: rewire ChatView navigate() calls to callbacks for column mode
description:
Five useNavigate() calls in ChatView.tsx navigate the whole window away from /columns when invoked from a column. Audit identified them at:

- ChatView.tsx:1042-1045 (PR dialog → draft) navigates to /draft/$draftId
- ChatView.tsx:1076-1079 (new draft thread) navigates to /draft/$draftId
- ChatView.tsx:1226 (Connections → reconnect) navigates to /settings/connections
- ChatView.tsx:1713-1724 (toggle diff panel) navigates to /$envId/$threadId
- ChatView.tsx:3329-3335 (Implement in new thread) navigates to /$envId/$threadId
- ChatView.tsx:3469-3481 (open turn diff) navigates to /$envId/$threadId

Each must check useColumnContext() (from ColumnChatSurface). If inside a column, route the action through a callback that updates columnsStateStore (e.g. bindColumn) and/or sets per-column UI state, instead of navigate(). If outside (regular thread route), keep existing navigate() behavior.

REPO: ~/t3 (branch feat/multi-agent-columns-scaffold)

Acceptance:
- Opening a turn diff in a column does not leave /columns
- Creating a new thread from a column either rebinds that column to the new thread, or opens it in the column slot specified by the user
- Reconnect / settings navigations stay in /columns (or open in a new tab if needed)

See: docs/research/2026-05-22-project-3-multi-column-scoped.md (M-col-1 audit findings)
notes:
Three of the highest-impact navigate calls rerouted to ColumnContext callbacks in ~/t3 commit 1b2196a3: onToggleDiff (1713), onOpenTurnDiff (3469), onImplementPlanInNewThread (3329). ColumnContext extended with intent{rebindToThread, openDiff, closeDiff}; ChatView uses these when useColumnContext() is non-null, falls back to navigate() in single-thread mode. Diff state lives in columnsStateStore.diffStateByColumnId. Inline column-local DiffPanel rendering deferred — ColumnChatSurface shows a placeholder badge for now. Two remaining draft-navigate calls (1042, 1076) deferred; draft state differs from server-thread state.
Inline DiffPanel feasibility: DiffPanel.tsx (743 lines) is heavily URL-coupled — reads routeThreadRef via useParams(), selectedTurnId/filePath via useSearch(diff,diffTurnId,diffFilePath), and uses useNavigate() to update selection. Can't be mounted in a column without either (a) refactoring DiffPanel to accept props { threadRef, turnId, filePath, onChange* } with the existing URL version becoming a thin bridge (architecturally sound, big change), or (b) building a slimmer <ColumnDiffPanel> that reuses sub-pieces (@pierre/diffs FileDiff, parsePatchFiles, etc.) but has its own prop-driven state model. The placeholder badge in ColumnChatSurface (with turnId + filePath shown) is the right v0 until one of these lands. Recommend path (a) — cleaner long term, the prop bridge in the existing route is ~10 lines.

title: gt: polecat-work formula — REPO: frontmatter validated at sling time
description:
**Target: the `mol-polecat-work` formula (Gas Town gt-tooling, not a
specific rig). This bead is filed in `review_pdf_to_latex` for tracking
but the patch lives wherever the formula schema is defined.**

Status: P2, mayor-approved (hq-wisp-4s1, thread-37c8743ce1a8). Mayor
will review when the patch lands.

## Background — the failure

On 2026-05-22, three polecats (rust/chrome/nitro) were slung off the
review_pdf_to_latex rig to work on agent-viewer (~/PycharmProjects/agent-viewer
— a separate repo from the rig's home repo).

- rust succeeded — PR #1 landed on github.com/AJBcoding/agent-viewer.
- chrome (rev-si7) and nitro (rev-cba) both **false-closed in ~80 seconds**
  with reason "Completed with no code changes (already fixed or pushed
  directly to main)" — referencing commit_sha 67f35db1f7..., which is a
  docs(handoff) commit on the **rig's home repo**, not agent-viewer.

Root cause (mayor confirmed): the formula's "no changes needed → close"
path honored the polecat's reading of its rig worktree without verifying
the polecat had checked the **intended** repo. Polecats correctly
operated on their assigned worktree; the formula's mistake was trusting
their conclusion without the cwd guardrail.

Re-filed beads (rev-34c, rev-0ey) with explicit "REPO: …" as the FIRST
paragraph + step-by-step `cd` Setup section. Both polecats then worked
in the right repo. Bead description format CAN solve this — formula
validation makes it consistent.

## Proposal

Add a required `REPO:` (or `repo:`) frontmatter field to the polecat-work
formula's bead description schema. Validated at `gt sling` time:

```
$ gt sling rev-NEW-1 review_pdf_to_latex --create
✗ Bead rev-NEW-1 is missing required `REPO:` frontmatter.
  Expected format on the first line of the description:
    REPO: <absolute path or repo-relative pointer>
  Aborting sling. Use `bd update rev-NEW-1 --append-notes` to add it.
```

Validation logic (small):
1. Parse the first non-empty paragraph of the bead description.
2. Look for `REPO:` (or `Repo:`, case-insensitive).
3. Expand `~` to $HOME. Resolve to absolute path.
4. Assert directory exists. Optional: assert `.git` exists if the
   `polecat-work` formula is expected to produce a commit.
5. Surface the resolved path in the convoy entry + the polecat's hook
   announcement so the operator sees it.

If REPO is omitted, default to "this rig's home repo" (current
behavior) — preserves backwards compat for in-rig work.

## Why this is the right primary fix

- **Fails loud at sling time**, not silently 80s later.
- Single field for reviewers to scan when a bead lands cross-repo.
- Pure schema + validation work — no spawn-step plumbing changes.
- Forward-compatible with proposal (1) below: once REPO is required, a
  follow-up patch can have the spawn step `cd $REPO` before handing off.

## Related proposals (for the formula author's eyes)

(1) **`--cwd <abs-path>` formula_var.** Bigger. Touches the spawn step
    of `mol-polecat-work`. Right secondary fix after (2) lands.
    Probably warrants an ADR on whether cross-rig sling is intended at
    all — today it's "yes by accident."

(3) **Self-report before close.** Orthogonal hardening: any time the
    formula takes the "no changes needed" close path, require the
    polecat to first report `looked_in=<path>, grepped_for=<pattern>`.
    Would have surfaced today's chrome/nitro mismatch immediately. Cheap.
    Fold into the formula regardless of (1)/(2).

## The rust puzzle (note for the formula author)

Rust faced the exact same cwd default as chrome/nitro and still managed
to operate in the correct repo (~/PycharmProjects/agent-viewer), commit
as "rust <kraken-11.sitcoms@icloud.com>", push to a feature branch, and
open PR #1. Chrome and nitro on the same defaults bailed. Possibilities:

- rust took the bead's `Repo: ~/PycharmProjects/agent-viewer` line more
  literally (prompt-interpretation luck)
- something in rust's bead description was prompt-shaped differently
  (compare rev-x39 vs rev-si7 verbatim — should be similar templates)
- non-determinism in formula execution that happens to surface only on
  cwd ambiguity

Worth a quick A/B before designing the fix to make sure REPO:
validation closes the actual gap and not just the visible one.

## Acceptance

- `gt sling <bead>` rejects bead descriptions without a REPO: line,
  with a clear error message + remediation steps.
- A bead description with `REPO: ~/PycharmProjects/agent-viewer`
  resolves correctly (tilde expansion, directory existence check).
- Existing in-rig beads (REPO unset) continue to default to the rig's
  home repo with no schema break.
- Convoy/hook surface the resolved REPO so the operator sees it.
- Brief test: file a bead targeting a non-existent path, sling, expect
  rejection.

## Sizing

~1 day for the patch + tests. Mostly schema + validation logic + a
test in the formula's test suite.

## Cross-references

- Mayor approval thread: hq-wisp-4s1 (Re: Tier 3 cwd mismatch — formula
  hardening recommendation)
- Original false-close exemplars: rev-si7 (chrome), rev-cba (nitro)
- Recovery beads (worked): rev-34c (chrome), rev-0ey (nitro)
- Mayor's parallel "didn't use polecats" retro: hq-jxsa4 (same theme:
  cross-cutting work doesn't survive default-mode reflexes)

title: agent-viewer: streaming partial assistant messages
description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-kcas

## Goal

Enable live "typing" of assistant messages — text appears chunk-by-chunk
as the SDK emits partials, instead of arriving as one complete block per
turn. Matches t3's MessagesTimeline streaming behaviour.

## Repo / branch

- Repo: ~/PycharmProjects/agent-viewer (separate from this rig — see
  github.com/AJBcoding/agent-viewer)
- Branch fresh from main.

## Files to touch

- `src/main/claude-backend.ts` — pass `includePartialMessages: true` to
  query options (verify exact field name in SDK; might be on Options
  type or set via env flag passthrough).
- `src/shared/adapter.ts` — handle SDKPartialAssistantMessage event
  type. Currently the `default` case ignores it (line ~250). Add a case
  that emits a `message` BackendEvent with the partial text and the
  SAME uuid as the eventual full assistant message so the store can
  merge.
- `src/renderer/src/store.ts` — `appendOrdered` currently treats
  duplicate-id as overwrite-in-place (correct for streaming). Verify
  the text merges rather than replaces — may need to handle "partial
  appends to existing" vs "full replaces partial." T3's pattern
  (apps/web/src/store.ts:1370-1458) is:
    text = streaming ? `${entry.text}${message.text}` : message.text
  Adopt that. Add a `streaming?: boolean` field to ChatMessage already
  per shared/types.ts — populate it on partials.
- `src/renderer/src/components/MessageBubble.tsx` — optional: blink
  cursor or fade-in if streaming. Match t3's WorkingTimelineRow
  staggered-dot pattern but inline at the cursor.
- `src/shared/adapter.test.ts` — add fixture for a streaming sequence
  (multiple partials with same uuid + a final full message) and assert
  the adapter emits the correct events.

## Acceptance

- Send a prompt that triggers a long response. Text appears
  incrementally over ~1-3s rather than as a single block at the end.
- No duplicate message bubbles. The same message-id merges in place.
- WorkingIndicator disappears as soon as the first partial lands
  (currently it disappears when busy=false, which is on turnDone;
  for streaming we want it to disappear when first assistant chunk
  arrives).
- 10/10 adapter tests still pass; new streaming test passes.

## Sizing

~1.5 days. Most of the time is in the adapter+store merge logic and
verifying edge cases (partial then interrupted, partial then tool_use,
etc.).

## References

- Compare doc: ~/PycharmProjects/review-pdf-to-latex/docs/research/2026-05-22-agent-viewer-vs-t3-comparison.md (section "Streaming")
- T3 reference: ~/t3/apps/web/src/store.ts:1370-1458 (case "thread.message-sent")

- adapter.ts: case "stream_event" handles content_block_delta(text_delta);
  emits streaming:true ChatMessage with same uuid as eventual full message.
  Non-text deltas (thinking/json/citations) and content_block_start/stop
  framing ignored.
- claude-backend.ts: query options now include includePartialMessages:true
- store.ts: mergeMessage helper — concat text when existing.streaming &&
  incoming.streaming, replace otherwise. Preserves existing createdAt.
- types.ts: added streaming?: boolean to ChatMessage
- MessageBubble.tsx + styles.css: blinking caret while streaming
- WorkingIndicator: NO change needed — existing showWorking heuristic in

  last entry becomes an assistant message, which happens on first partial.

title: [bug] Claude pane: Enter/Return hangs after toolbar prompt submit (Create Context / Sling / Fresh Start)
description: AJB during M7 dev-server verification: 'on the first round they were hanging there' — after typing a prompt in the Create Context (or another toolbar) modal and submitting, the Claude session appears to hang. Likely the modal's prompt-write to the worker pty is missing the trailing newline/CR or not flushing to the pty before the modal closes.
notes:
Investigation:
1. desktop/renderer/toolbar.ts — find where the Create Context / Sling modals submit their prompt to the spawned worker pty
2. Check whether the prompt write ends with '' (CR — what Claude expects from a TTY) or '
'; node-pty on macOS wants ''
3. Check whether the modal closes BEFORE pty:workerInput resolves; if the renderer tears down the IPC bridge mid-write, the input lands in limbo
4. Compare against the working conversational-pty path (claude-pane.ts) which writes '/review-pdf-to-latex' successfully

Surfaced by AJB during M7 verification walkthrough; observed on rev-1md.3 toolbar modals.
close_reason:
Root cause + fix landed this session.

ROOT CAUSE: worker pty's multi-line priming was sent line-by-line with \r between every line (claude-pty.ts:548-555). Claude's TUI treats bare \r as 'submit the current buffer' — so each line submitted as its own user turn. First line went to claude; while claude was responding, subsequent lines piled into the input queue producing the 'Enter hangs after first round' symptom.

FIX: switched to xterm bracketed-paste mode (ESC[200~ ... ESC[201~ \r) so the entire multi-line prompt arrives as a single paste, with newlines treated as soft newlines and only the trailing \r committing. New writeBracketedPaste(p, text) helper in desktop/main/claude-pty.ts. Also applied defensively to the conversational pty's primingExtra path for the (rare) multi-line freshStart handoff case.

Verification: typecheck passes. Test in dev server: Create Context modal with multi-line prompt → worker pty should fire ONE response to the full bundle, not N partial responses.

title: [bug] §9.2.3 slash-command priming not visible in Claude pane scrollback at first spawn
description: AJB during M7 dev-server verification: couldn't see the first line '/review-pdf-to-latex' that §9.2.3 (per the rev-z8i patch) says should be injected on every spawn. Either: (a) the priming isn't actually firing, (b) it's firing but rendered without visible scrollback, (c) it's firing too early before the xterm DOM is mounted.
notes:

title: [bug] right-drawer comments don't restore on doc reopen even though PDF highlights do
description: Discovered during dev-server verification of M7 (commit 077a432). When the user reopens a previously-reviewed PDF, the PDF.js highlight overlays appear correctly but the right-drawer comment cards are empty. Drafts file at .review-state/drafts/<sha256>.json should be loaded and rendered as cards.
notes:
Reproduction (from AJB walkthrough):
1. Open a PDF, make some highlight+comment cards
2. Quit app or close PDF
3. Reopen the same PDF
4. PDF-side highlights restore (correct)
5. Right-drawer cards: EMPTY (BUG)

Likely causes to investigate:
- loadDraftsForCurrentDoc in desktop/renderer/index.ts may bail if doc sha256 doesn't match drafts.doc_version (rev-1md.2 may have changed this)
- ScheduleDraftsWrite may have stopped firing after rev-1md.1's bundle writer integration
- The user may have opened a different file than they created comments on (e.g., the v1.1 revision file rather than v1.0 — those have different sha256s)

First step: open the dev-server window, open a doc, make a card, check that .review-state/drafts/<sha256>.json exists and has the comment. Then reopen and check whether loadDraftsForCurrentDoc fires and returns data.
2026-05-23: Folded into rev-mf3 M-md-0. Root cause is sha256-keyed sidecar identity — when PDF bytes change (re-export, re-save) the sha256 changes and sidecar lookup misses. M-md-0 migrates all sidecars from sha256 to path-mirroring + content-fingerprint fallback, which fixes this for PDFs as a side effect. See docs/research/2026-05-23-md-review-scoped.md § "Q5 sub-decision" and § "M-md-0" acceptance criteria.

title: contract: gt mail Submit payload + delivery confirmation + concurrent-round Submit lock
description: rev-1md.4 Submit flow has three load-bearing gaps the spec hand-waves: (1) gt mail payload shape isn't pinned (handoff calls this out as known-unknown), (2) gt mail is fire-and-forget — 'submitted, awaiting rig' has no timeout/retry/proof-of-delivery, (3) concurrent-round Submit isn't blocked (user can fire Cmd+Return twice in 200ms, or submit round 2 while round 1 is mid-process). Surfaced by Pass 4 of the M7 review (top ship blockers).
notes:
Three sub-deliverables, all pre-rev-1md.4:

A. PAYLOAD SHAPE
Pin the exact 'gt mail send' payload structure for Submit. Per §10.1 step 3 the payload carries: submit_file path, bundle_pdf path, bundle_json path, source_doc path, origin_rig id, bundle_id. Confirm whether gt mail supports structured attachments or whether we serialize JSON into the message body. Document in a small spec patch under §10.1.

B. DELIVERY CONFIRMATION
Define a state machine for the 'awaiting rig' state in the desktop app:
- pending_send (Submit fired, gt mail not exited yet)
- sent_unconfirmed (gt mail exit 0, no rig ack)
- acknowledged (rig wrote .review-state/round-<bundle-id>/ack or first results entry)
- timeout (e.g., 10 min in sent_unconfirmed → show resubmit affordance)
- send_failed (gt mail exit != 0, surface stderr)
Surface gt stderr verbatim on failure rather than swallowing it.

C. CONCURRENT-ROUND LOCK
When .review-state/results-<ts>.json exists with round_status: in_progress for the current doc, disable Cmd+Return (and the toolbar Submit button if added later). Show the resume banner from §10.1 step 6 INSTEAD of a fresh Submit. Add an explicit 'abandon this round' affordance with age-check (don't show resume for results files older than e.g. 7 days without an explicit user opt-in).

Blocks rev-1md.4.
SUBMIT CONTRACT DESIGN — gt mail payload + delivery state machine + concurrent-round lock

A. PAYLOAD CONTRACT

Verbatim invocation (rig case; standalone-to-Reviewer is identical with 'reviewer/' address):

  gt mail send "<origin_rig>/" \
    --type task --priority 1 --permanent \
    --subject "review-pdf submit · <base>-<source_version> · <bundle_id>" \
    --stdin <<'BODY'
  {
    "schema_version": 1,
    "kind": "review-pdf.submit",
    "app_version": "0.x.y",
    "bundle_id": "20260520-192300",
    "submit_id": "20260520-192300",
    "origin_rig": "report-engine/anthony",
    "destination_rig": "report-engine/anthony",
    "source_doc": "/abs/path/report-1.0.pdf",
    "submit_file": "/abs/path/.review-state/submit-20260520-192300.json",
    "bundle_pdf":  "/abs/path/2026-05-20 report-1.0 (AJB edits).pdf",
    "bundle_json": "/abs/path/2026-05-20 report-1.0 (AJB edits).json",
    "expected_skill": "/review-pdf process",
    "submitted_at": "2026-05-20T19:23:00Z"
  }
  BODY

Why: gt mail send has no attachment/structured-body surface — body is a single string, so we use --stdin (avoids shell-quoting on paths with spaces from the bundle filename grammar). All artifacts referenced by absolute path; rig + app share filesystem (gt assumption). Address is broadcast <rig>/, not skill-specific (gt routes to agents not skills); rig decides whether to run /review-pdf process based on expected_skill + subject prefix — soft contract the rig can rename without breaking wire. --type task = required processing; --priority 1 = user actively waiting; --permanent = recoverable if rig session restarts.

B. DELIVERY STATE MACHINE

States: idle → pending_send → sent_unconfirmed → acknowledged → processing → complete (then → idle), plus send_failed and timeout sidebranches.

Transitions:
- submit-click: idle → pending_send (UI hard-locks Cmd+Return at IPC boundary)
- gt-exit-0: pending_send → sent_unconfirmed
- gt-exit-≠0: pending_send → send_failed (display verbatim stderr; [Retry] affordance)
- results-<bundle_id>.json appears: sent_unconfirmed → acknowledged
- first results[] entry written: acknowledged → processing (banner refines)
- round_status: complete: processing → complete (then auto-clear in 5s → idle)
- round_status: failed: processing → complete-failed (persistent banner)
- 10min elapsed in sent_unconfirmed: timeout banner overlay (still in sent_unconfirmed; [Re-sling] + [Show gt mail status] affordances)
- 30s elapsed in pending_send: pending_send → send_failed (gt mail should never hang locally)

Ack mechanism: NO separate ack file. The results-<bundle_id>.json's existence with round_status: in_progress + results: [] IS the ack; first results[] entry is the heartbeat. Matches §10.3 already; no new contract surface needed on the rig side.

UI per state (uses §5.2's non-blocking banner pattern):
- idle: title-bar 'Saved' indicator only
- pending_send: spinner pill 'Slinging to <rig>…'
- sent_unconfirmed: pill 'Submitted to <rig> — awaiting pickup'
- sent_unconfirmed + 10min timeout: pill + sibling banner 'Still waiting (10 min). [Re-sling] [Show gt mail status]'
- acknowledged/processing: 'Round in progress — N of M comments processed' (matches §10.1 step 6)
- complete: toast 'Round complete — N applied, M failed. [View results]' auto-clear 5s
- complete-failed: persistent banner 'Round failed — see results'
- send_failed: persistent error banner with verbatim gt stderr + [Retry]

Timeout values: gt-mail-exit-deadline 30s (local op), sent_unconfirmed → timeout-banner 10min, complete toast 5s.

C. CONCURRENT-ROUND LOCK

Rules:
1. Submit enabled iff no results-*.json for current doc_version has round_status: in_progress AND in-memory state in {idle, complete, send_failed, timeout}.
2. In pending_send + sent_unconfirmed: Cmd+Return hard-disabled at IPC boundary (not just visually). Belt-and-braces debounce on the IPC side covers double-fire-in-200ms.
3. On doc open, scan .review-state/ for results-*.json matching current submit_id/doc_version:
   - Age ≤ 7 days AND round_status: in_progress → replace Submit button with 'Resume round in progress' banner; Cmd+Return becomes 'focus the rig' instead of fresh sling.
   - Age > 7 days AND round_status: in_progress → 'Previous round didn't finish (started <date>, N of M processed) — [Resume] [Abandon and start fresh]'. No auto-resume; explicit user choice.
4. 'Send to same rig again' after a completed round is NOT a concurrent-round case; that's a fresh round on a new doc_version (§10.1 step 6 seeds next draft).

Abandon affordance:
- Resume banner carries [Abandon round] secondary action.
- Stale (>7d) banner makes Abandon co-equal with Resume.
- Semantics: rename results-<ts>.json → results-<ts>.abandoned.json (preserves audit trail). Rig resume guard ignores .abandoned suffix. Flip in-memory state to idle, re-enable Submit. DO NOT DELETE — partial results may include real L3 dispositions worth consulting.

Stale-detection rule: file mtime older than 7 days AND round_status: in_progress = stale; different banner variant. A true mid-flight round resolves within hours; a week-old in_progress almost certainly means the rig session was abandoned and the user shouldn't get a one-click resume that might do something surprising.

Spec patches landing: §10.1 step 3 (gt mail invocation + envelope), §10.1 step 4 (state machine + timeouts), §10.1 step 6 (Submit-lock + abandon + stale rule), §10.3 (.abandoned suffix as soft tombstone).

Unblocks rev-1md.4 (Submit flow) and rev-1md.5 (results watcher).
close_reason: Contract design recorded as note. Spec patches landed in §10.1 steps 3/4/6: gt mail invocation, delivery state machine with timeouts, concurrent-round lock + abandon semantics. Implementation work folds into rev-1md.4 (Submit) and rev-1md.5 (results watcher).

title: pick: PDF annotation color palette (L1/L2/L3) + right-drawer card colors
description: Spec §10.4 + §13.19 + §9.1 all reference an engagement-level color palette but none define it. §10.4 PDF rendering needs colors for Highlight annotations (one per L1/L2/L3); §9.1 right-drawer cards should match for visual cue consistency. Currently a load-bearing TBD that blocks rev-1md.1's annotation output. Surfaced by Pass 3 of the M7 review.
notes:
Decide a 3-color palette that:

1. Renders correctly in Preview, Acrobat, and iOS Quick Look (the §10.4 'human-portable' claim).
2. Is distinguishable for color-blind users (avoid red-green-only contrast for L1/L3).
3. Sets popup color (color name in PDF /C array, 0-1 floats) AND right-drawer card border/chip color (CSS).

Default suggestion: L1 Comment = soft yellow (#FFE066 / [1.0, 0.88, 0.4]), L2 Redraft = soft blue (#7FB3FF / [0.498, 0.702, 1.0]), L3 Surface = soft red/coral (#FF8A8A / [1.0, 0.541, 0.541]). Validate against the three readers before locking.

Closes §13.19. Blocks rev-1md.1.
PALETTE DECISION (proposed; pending one-page smoke-test PDF across Preview / Acrobat / iOS Quick Look)

L1 Comment:
  hex: #F5C84B
  rgb: [245, 200, 75]
  pdf /C: [0.961, 0.784, 0.294]
  css: --level-l1-bg: #F5C84B1A; --level-l1-border: #F5C84B
  rationale: Warm amber — reads as "highlighter yellow" in every PDF reader and matches §11.1 "fix this, here's why" → small-fix / warm-zone semantic.

L2 Redraft:
  hex: #6FB1FF
  rgb: [111, 177, 255]
  pdf /C: [0.435, 0.694, 1.000]
  css: --level-l2-bg: #6FB1FF1A; --level-l2-border: #6FB1FF
  rationale: Sky-blue — neutral/cool, reads as "swap this" without alarm; sits adjacent to existing app --accent (#4a9eff).

L3 Surface:
  hex: #E26FB1
  rgb: [226, 111, 177]
  pdf /C: [0.886, 0.435, 0.694]
  css: --level-l3-bg: #E26FB11A; --level-l3-border: #E26FB1
  rationale: Magenta-pink — high-attention without using true red; separates cleanly from L1 amber for deuteranopes/protanopes (red↔green is the dominant CVD axis).

Opacity for PDF highlights: /CA 0.5 (Preview/Acrobat default for Highlight subtype; identical in iOS Quick Look).

Existing styles.css audit:
- L1/L2/L3 chip colors live at .comment-section-label (~L393-395), .comment-card border-left (~L417-419), .comment-card-level text (~L433-435). Currently hardcoded to #4a9eff / #c084fc / #38bdf8 (blue/purple/cyan — arbitrary, not semantic).
- Clean path: add --level-l{1,2,3}-color CSS variables to :root at top of styles.css; replace the 9 hardcoded hex literals with var() refs. rev-1md.1's bundle writer imports the same constants from a shared engagement-palette.ts so PDF and UI stay lockstep.

title: schema migration: add pdf_annotation_id to CommentPayload + drafts file
description: Spec §8 added pdf_annotation_id linking comments to bundle PDF annotations. The field is absent from desktop/shared/types.ts CommentPayload. rev-1md.1 needs to either extend the schema (touching existing drafts via a migration) or punt the field — but the field is what makes the bundle PDF round-trippable, so punting defeats §10.4's degraded-restore path. Surfaced by Pass 1 of the M7 review.
notes:
Acceptance criteria:

1. Add 'pdf_annotation_id?: string' (optional) to CommentPayload in desktop/shared/types.ts.
2. Bump DraftsFile schema_version (1 → 2) and add a one-way migration in main: when reading a v1 drafts file, treat all pdf_annotation_id as undefined; on next write, schema_version becomes 2. Old drafts continue to load.
3. The id is set by the bundle writer (rev-1md.1) when an annotation lands in the bundle PDF — it is NOT set by the renderer at comment-creation time. Live drafts can carry it null/undefined.
4. Update the bundle JSON schema example in spec §10.4 to reflect this if not already aligned.

Blocks rev-1md.1.
SCHEMA MIGRATION DESIGN

1. Type definitions (desktop/shared/types.ts):

   CommentPayload gains:
     pdf_annotation_id?: string;
   Set ONLY by the bundle writer (rev-1md.1) at bundle-write time. NEVER populated by the renderer at comment-creation time. `undefined` means "not yet bundled"; `null` is reserved (treat as undefined on read, never write).

   DraftsFile shape:
     export interface DraftsFileV1 { schema_version: 1; doc_version: string; comments: CommentPayload[]; }
     export interface DraftsFileV2 { schema_version: 2; doc_version: string; comments: CommentPayload[]; }
     export type DraftsFile = DraftsFileV2;
     export type DraftsFileAny = DraftsFileV1 | DraftsFileV2;
   Renderer + IPC always speak the latest (v2). Main upgrades on read.

2. Migration strategy: read-time upgrade in main.
   drafts:read parses raw JSON as DraftsFileAny; if schema_version === 1, rewrites as { schema_version: 2, doc_version, comments } (pdf_annotation_id stays undefined). Next debounced drafts:write persists v2 via existing atomic temp+rename. Renderer stays monomorphic; never branches on schema_version.

3. Who sets pdf_annotation_id: bundle writer only; do NOT backfill the live draft.
   Bundle is a one-way derivative of the draft (§10.4). Backfilling would (1) race with user's next keystroke, (2) churn the drafts file with IDs meaningful only to the bundle, (3) couple bundle's id-generation strategy to drafts file stability. rev-1md.1 holds a Map<comment_id, annotation_id> in memory during bundle write, emits both PDF annotations + JSON sidecar from that map, discards it.

4. Round-trip preservation: bundle ↔ submit only, deliberately not threaded through the version bump.
   Chain: bundle JSON comments[].pdf_annotation_id (app, §10.4) → submit file comments[].pdf_annotation_id (verbatim; §10.3 line 502 already shows it) → rig reads submit+bundle (§10.1 step 5) → results file is status-only by `id`, not annotation-keyed → seeded v1.1 draft (deferred/needs-followup/build_failed) gets fresh UUIDs and undefined pdf_annotation_id. v1.0 bundle is the audit anchor; v1.1 earns its own id when its own bundle is written.

5. Defensive cases:
   - v1 with stray pdf_annotation_id (hand-edit): keep on upgrade, log warning. No data loss.
   - v2 with pdf_annotation_id: null vs missing: convention is "missing means not bundled" (canonical); null tolerated on read, never written.
   - Unknown schema_version: return parse_failed (renderer flashes "drafts load failed") rather than silently dropping comments. Mirrors the AppStateFile guard at main/index.ts:515.
   - schema_version absent: treat as parse_failed.

6. Bundle JSON schema placement: per-comment (comments[].pdf_annotation_id).
   §10.4 sketch says comments[] uses full §8 schema; §10.3 line 502 already embeds it inline; §8 treats it as one row in the comment model. A bundle-level annotation_index would split one fact across two locations, require joins, and introduce orphan-entry failure modes. Per-comment placement also keeps §10.4 degraded-restore symmetric: both PDF and JSON key the same way.
close_reason: Design recorded as note: optional pdf_annotation_id field; read-time upgrade in main from schema_version 1→2; bundle writer is sole populator; per-comment placement in bundle JSON. Implementation work folds into rev-1md.1.

title: decide + scope: comment kind enum (highlight/strikethrough/standalone) vs v1 highlights-only
description: Spec §5.1 lists three PDF comment kinds (highlight, strikethrough, standalone) and §10.4's bundle rendering requires them. The current CommentPayload (desktop/shared/types.ts:107-121) only carries 'kind: comment' (a literal), and the renderer (pdf-viewer.ts) only emits text-selection highlights. rev-1md.1 cannot produce strikethrough or sticky-note annotations as written. Surfaced by the M7 4-pass design review (Pass 1).
notes:
Two options:

A. EXTEND now — broaden kind enum to {'highlight','strikethrough','standalone'}, extend the renderer to capture the latter two (text-strike gesture, click-to-anchor for standalone), update CommentPayload + drafts schema. Larger scope but unblocks the §10.4 contract as written.

B. SCOPE DOWN — pin v1 bundle to highlights-only, mark strikethrough + standalone explicitly out-of-scope for M7 in §10.4 and §5.1, file a follow-up bd for v2. Smaller, ships faster, but the §10.4 spec text needs to match the implementation.

Recommendation: B for M7 (lowest churn, matches what the renderer actually emits today), with a follow-up bd for the kind-enum extension. Decide before rev-1md.1 starts so the bundle writer doesn't carry dead code-paths.

Blocks rev-1md.1.
DECISION: option B (scope down to highlights-only for M7)

Why:
Current renderer is a text-selection capture machine: PDF.js TextLayer + Range.getClientRects → SelectionPayload always carries non-empty highlighted_text. The two missing kinds need genuinely different input gestures:
- Strikethrough: same selection, different annotation type — but there's no tool palette slot for it (§4.2 has Comment/Redraft/Surface; the second-Redraft-vs-strikethrough question is itself open).
- Standalone: click-to-anchor gesture, region-less anchor shape — a real renderer feature.

Doing both now means: tool-palette redesign, new gesture handlers in pdf-viewer.ts, anchor-shape variants in CommentPayload, drafts schema_version bump + migration, matching pdf-lib annotation paths in bundle writer. None required for M7's actual outcome (round-trip handoff). Highlights-only validates the loop end-to-end on AJB's real workflow; extending afterward gets us evidence about which gestures matter.

Concrete consequences:
- rev-1md.1 implements only highlight kind; pdf-lib emits Highlight annotations only
- CommentPayload.kind stays narrow ('comment' literal); drafts stays schema_version 1 (interacts with rev-ni4 which bumps it to 2 for pdf_annotation_id — that bump is unrelated to kind)
- Tool palette stays as-is (§4.2 second-Redraft question stays open)
- Standalone-comment users must anchor to selected text in v1 (acceptable per AJB's stated review-against-source workflow)

Spec patches landed: §5.1 + §10.4 (this commit).

Follow-up bd: filed as rev-... ("v2 — strikethrough + standalone comment kinds"); links back to this decision.
close_reason: Decision: option B (highlights-only for M7). Spec patches landed in §5.1 + §10.4. v2 follow-up filed as rev-6nr.

title: skill: /review rig-side launcher + /review-pdf process round-based processor
description: Author the rig-side skills that complete the Submit handoff loop. Per spec §13.18 — sketched in spec but full skill text needs to be written.
notes:
Two skills, both in ~/.claude/skills/:

1. /review (rig-side launcher)
   - Invoked from a crew rig: '/review <path-to-doc>'
   - Knows its own rig identity (it's running in it)
   - Spawns the Electron app via the CLI shim: 'review-pdf-app open <path> --from <rig-id>'
   - Returns control to the rig session

2. /review-pdf process (rig-side processor)
   - Input: absolute path to .review-state/submit-<ts>.json
   - Reads submit file + bundle JSON
   - Prompts: easy work first (L1+L2) or surface first (L3)? — smart default of easy-first; user can override (per §10.1 step 5)
   - For each L1/L2: apply → build → on build failure, revert + mark build_failed (per-comment retry-then-skip, Gap 3 (a'))
   - For L3: pause, ask user conversationally (sequential in document order; skip-ahead allowed; graceful interrupt)
   - Writes results-<ts>.json incrementally (per-comment atomic append, round_status: in_progress)
   - At round end: prompts version bump (§10.6) — minor (default) / major / custom validated against \d+\.\d+
   - Writes new versioned source file
   - Finalizes results file (round_status: complete, new_source_path, version_chosen)
   - Single git commit per §10.6 message shape

3. /review-pdf redraft (live-redraft variant, §10.2)
   - Wrapper over /review-pdf process for a single-item bundle in non-mutating mode
   - No file edits, no build, no commit
   - Writes redraft_suggestion back into the live draft

Implementation orchestrates existing engine atomic subcommands: apply, build, revert, preview. Legacy 4-phase subcommands (commit-phase, serve, wait-event, etc.) are NOT used; tracked for removal in rev-y0r.

title: desktop: flush drafts on app quit + window unload
description:
Drafts are written 250ms debounced (per spec §10.3). If the user Cmd+Q's the app within 250ms of submitting a comment, the pending write never fires and the comment is lost. In-memory state is authoritative until the next successful write, so the comment exists in this session — it just doesn't survive the quit.

Fix: bind `window.addEventListener('beforeunload', flushDraftsWrite)` in the renderer + add an `app.on('before-quit')` handler in main that awaits the renderer's flush before letting the process exit. Renderer side already exposes `flushDraftsWrite` at module scope.

Discovered: milestone #4 (commit cc8ccdd). See handoff docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md, limitation #1.
notes: Implemented flush handshake (main → renderer) on app before-quit + BrowserWindow close, plus beforeunload fallback. Verified by /tmp/verify-cm6.js: comment submitted then app.close() within 76-91ms persists to drafts file. Scenario C (no pending writes) shuts down cleanly without spurious empty drafts file.

title: engine: 'surface-first' order should be the real Phase 1 default when surface-intent dominates
description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-td2
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T21:13:37Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Real-world observation from the COTA run: of 8 unresolved annotations remaining after auto-deferral, 6 were surface-intent (structural/META) and only 2 were clean mechanical applies. Running Phase 1 batch on the 2 mechanicals BEFORE walking the 6 surfaces felt backward — the human wanted the surfaces handled first because those conversations would inform whether the mechanicals were still wanted at all.

The engine already exposes `--order surface-first` on `serve` (per spec §9.5), but:
- Phase 1's mechanical batch fires before either ordering's Phase 2 walk, regardless of --order.
- pending → surfaced_pending may not be a permitted transition (the reporter was about to test); if it isn't, the user has no way to opt a pending annotation into Phase 2b routing before the mechanical pass.

Fix shape:

title: extract: spatially associate free-floating sticky notes with nearest highlight
description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-4hl
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T21:24:07Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

When a reviewer adds a sticky note (Text annotation) at a location near a highlight — instead of using Acrobat's 'right-click highlighted text → Add note' which keeps them as one annotation — the engine sees two separate annotations:

1. A Highlight with text but no comment.
2. A Text/sticky-note annotation with the comment but no underlying highlighted_text (just a bbox at the click point).

The current engine treats these as independent. Result: the highlighted text and the comment about it are split across two annotations, and the comment annotation falls into needs_review with no anchor text. The reviewer's intent ("this comment is ABOUT that highlight") is lost.

Fix: after read_annotations, run a post-processing pass that, for each Text annotation with no highlighted_text:
- Find Highlight annotations on the same page whose bbox is within N points of the sticky's bbox center (tune N — start at ~50pt, roughly two lines).
- If a unique winner exists, merge: copy highlighted_text from the Highlight onto the Text annotation (or vice-versa), preserving both bboxes for the viewer's overlay.
- If ambiguous (multiple highlights nearby), leave separate — the manual-mapping UI handles it.

Open design questions:
- Merge into one annotation, or keep two and just cross-link via a new `related_to` field?
  - Cross-link is less destructive but adds schema surface.
  - Merge is simpler downstream but loses the sticky note's original position.
- Distance metric: bbox-center Euclidean, or vertical-stripe nearest-on-the-left?
- How to handle sticky notes whose author wrote the comment that BELONGS to a SEPARATE highlight elsewhere on the page? (Probably out of scope — that's freeform user intent we can't infer.)

Source: AJB conversation 2026-05-17, about Acrobat's separate commenting/highlighting layers. Companion to rev-fv6 (bbox text recovery) which solved the underlying-highlight-text-is-empty case but NOT the comment-and-highlight-are-separate-annotations case.
notes:
Design decisions (resolving open questions):

1. **Merge vs cross-link**: Partial copy — copy `highlighted_text` from the
   matching Highlight onto the sticky's Annotation; leave the Highlight intact.
   Least-destructive: no schema change, both bboxes preserved (they live on
   separate Annotation objects), and the sticky now has an anchor that
   fuzzy_map can latch onto. The standalone Highlight (text, no comment) flows
   through normal mapping as before — pre-existing behavior, out of scope.

2. **Distance metric**: bbox-center Euclidean on the same page, threshold
   72pt (≈1 inch). Bead suggested 50pt; bumped to 72 because Acrobat's
   sticky-note glyph itself is ~16pt and reviewers click slightly off the
   highlighted text.

3. **Uniqueness rule**: nearest wins only if the runner-up is >= 1.5× as
   far. Otherwise leave separate (the existing needs_review manual-mapping
   UI handles ambiguous cases).

4. **Subtype detection**: use pdfannots `raw.subtype.name == "Text"` to
   identify sticky candidates and `"Highlight"` for anchor candidates. This
   is more precise than the "empty text + non-empty comment" heuristic
   (which would also catch genuine bbox-recovery failures on Highlights).

Companion to rev-fv6: rev-fv6 fixed the "Highlight has empty text" case via
bbox crop; this fixes the orthogonal "comment lives on a separate Text
annotation" case via spatial association.
close_reason: Closed

Running `extract` on a structurally-corrupt PDF (e.g., Adobe-edited PDF round-tripped through multiple tools) raises `AssertionError` from pdfannots at the first failing highlight with no traceback line numbers and no hint at what to do:

```
Missing text for Highlight annotation at page #2 (387.652,147.622)
ERROR: AssertionError:
```

Fix options:
- A. Wrap pdfannots in try/except and emit: 'Extraction failed: <reason>. The PDF may have corrupted content streams; try re-saving via Preview/Acrobat, or reconstructing annotations onto a clean copy.'
- B. Add a `--probe` flag that validates the PDF before extraction (count annotations, sample pages) and prints a structured report.
- C. Both.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BUG #6)

title: [review-docx] Word .docx review — convert to HTML preview, sidecar comments
description:
Child of rev-143. Order: ships last. .docx is the hardest format because it's binary (Office Open XML), needs a conversion pipeline, and round-tripping edits is genuinely difficult.

V1 scope (proposed): READ-ONLY review.
1. Conversion: mammoth.js (in-process, MIT, widely used) converts .docx → semantic HTML. Pandoc would give better fidelity but adds a binary dependency.
2. Rendering: pass mammoth's HTML output through the same surface built for rev-2h6 (html-viewer). Much of the work is already done at that point.
3. Anchoring: same as HTML (DOM Range / selector path). Note: round-tripping a comment-anchor back to the .docx's underlying XML for export is OUT of scope for v1.
4. Editing: NOT in v1. Users edit .docx in Word.
5. Track-changes / native Word comments: parse if mammoth exposes them; show as read-only metadata alongside our sidecar comments. Round-tripping our comments back AS Word comments is a much later effort.
6. Images embedded in .docx: mammoth extracts to data URLs or files — display in the HTML view.

Dependencies: rev-2h6 (HTML viewer infrastructure) must ship first to reuse rendering. mammoth.js (~150KB) adds to bundle size — acceptable.

Open questions:
- What's the right user mental model when a .docx changes on disk? PDF reopens silently; .docx after Word edits may need a 'reload from disk' affordance more obviously.
- Comment cards anchored to mammoth-converted HTML will break if mammoth output changes between sessions (e.g., library update). Need a stable anchor strategy — possibly paragraph index + char offset rather than DOM path.
notes: 2026-05-23: rev-mf3 scoped. This epic depends on rev-2h6 (HTML viewer), which itself depends on M-md-0 (rev-mf3.1 — FileViewer abstraction). The .docx conversion (mammoth.js → HTML) pipes through the same HTML rendering surface, so rev-2h6 must ship first. No direct dependency on .md-specific milestones M-md-1 through M-md-5.

title: [review-html] HTML review — rendered preview + source toggle
description:
Child of rev-143. Order: ships after rev-mf3 (.md). HTML shares the most surface area with .md (both are text-based, both render to a DOM), so much of the md-viewer infrastructure can be reused.

Decisions to make:
1. Rendering surface: <iframe sandbox> (safe, isolation, but resource-load complexity) vs. shadow DOM injection (lighter, more integrated, but XSS surface)? Default to iframe sandbox.
2. Source vs preview: same toggle pattern as .md (cmd+E).
3. Editable? Probably NOT for v1 — most .html in scope is generated output (LaTeX → HTML, exported pandoc) rather than hand-authored. Mark read-only initially, file follow-up if user wants editing.
4. Anchoring: DOM Range API (most stable for rendered view) or CSS selector path? Markdown uses line ranges; HTML's analog could be xpath or selector path.
5. Resource loading: relative paths to images/CSS — same dir as the .html file, sandbox needs allow-same-origin.
6. External resources (CDNs, scripts): block by default (sandbox).

Reuse from rev-mf3: viewer abstraction, drafts anchor schema (extend with anchor.kind='html-range'), source-vs-preview toggle UI.

Touch (provisional): new desktop/renderer/html-viewer.ts; FileKind needs 'html' added to types.ts; classifier in main/index.ts:57.
notes: 2026-05-23: rev-mf3 scoped. This epic depends specifically on M-md-0 (rev-mf3.1 — FileViewer abstraction + sidecar identity migration), not the entire rev-mf3 epic. Once M-md-0 lands, the FileViewer interface, path-mirrored sidecar scheme, and polymorphic anchor.kind are available for HTML to use. M-md-1 through M-md-5 (.md editing specifics) are not prerequisites for this epic.

title: [file-tree] search filter result UX — show only matches (flat list), not match-plus-ancestor context
