Spec §5.1 lists two additional PDF tools deferred from v1: Strikethrough (delete-this signal — same text-selection gesture, different annotation kind) and Standalone (click-to-anchor margin note, no highlight). When AJB hits a real 'delete this whole paragraph' or 'comment here, no specific text' need that highlights-only doesn't serve, this work fires.
notes:
v2 scope:

1. Extend CommentPayload.kind enum to 'highlight' | 'strikethrough' | 'standalone'. Bump drafts schema_version to 3 with one-shot migration: existing 'comment' literal → 'highlight'. (Coordinates with the schema_version 2 bump from rev-ni4 for pdf_annotation_id; v3 = both fields finalized.)

2. Add a Strikethrough tool to the §4.2 palette. Resolves the open question about the second Redraft slot — it becomes the Strikethrough slot.

3. Add click-to-anchor gesture in desktop/renderer/pdf-viewer.ts: capture a region-less SelectionPayload variant emitting only a point anchor (no highlighted_text), distinct from the current text-selection capture.

4. Extend §10.4 bundle writer to emit Strikethrough and Sticky-note annotations via pdf-lib alongside Highlight. Color palette per rev-pya extends to all three kinds × three engagement levels.

5. Update §5.1 + §10.4 spec text to reactivate the deferred bullets.

Driver: AJB needs the workflow expansion. Until then, anchored highlights cover the use case.

title: multi-user safety: bundle author + same-day overwrite collision detection
description: Spec §10.4 hardcodes the (AJB edits) suffix as v1-literal. Pass 4 of the M7 review flagged this as silently destructive the moment a second user (e.g., python419) opens the app: their Cmd+S overwrites AJB's bundle under AJB's name, and §10.4 rule (a) per-day-overwrite means intraday audit is gone too. Solo-user safe; blocks the moment any second user touches the app.
notes:
Acceptance criteria:

1. Read author from app settings (default 'AJB' for backward compat — derive from gh username, gt identity, or a one-time prompt at first launch).
2. Bundle filename interpolates the actual author: 'YYYY-MM-DD <base>-<v> (<author> edits).{pdf,json}'.
3. On bundle write, if a same-name bundle already exists, read its sidecar JSON's 'author' field. If different, refuse to overwrite — show a banner: 'A bundle by <other_author> already exists for today. Save under your own name?' with the user-author bundle written to a distinct path.
4. Multi-author session merge is OUT OF SCOPE for v1; multiple per-author bundles per day is the v1 answer.

Not a blocker for solo v1 ship. File now so it's visible before any second user onboards.

title: decide: Reviewer rig PATH/cwd restriction to enforce no-LaTeX-mutation
description: Spec §10.5.3 admits 'Reviewer never touches LaTeX — by design, not enforced.' Pass 4 of the M7 review flags this as a real foot-gun: §9.2.9 sets the pty cwd to the source dir of the currently-open PDF, which may contain .tex files. If pdflatex (or the engine) is on PATH inside the Reviewer rig, nothing prevents it from running. The 'by-convention' line is a hope, not a control.
notes:
Three options:

1. ACCEPT the risk for v1 (single-user AJB). File this issue as wontfix-for-v1, document the assumption in §10.5.3.
2. RESTRICT PATH when spawning the Reviewer-rig pty: strip pdflatex/xelatex/lualatex and the review-pdf engine binary from the inherited PATH. Cheap, defense-in-depth.
3. ENFORCE at skill level: the rig-side /review-pdf process skill (rev-1md.6) refuses to run when invoked under the Reviewer rig identity (it knows its own rig name from gt). The Reviewer can still launch ad-hoc claude conversations, but cannot ride the engine pipeline.

Recommendation: option 3 (skill-level enforcement). It's load-bearing for the §10.5.2 'L3-only' capability story and survives if the user mucks with PATH. Decide before rev-1md.6 starts.

Blocks rev-1md.6 (rig skills); soft-blocks rev-1md.2 (Reviewer rig spawning).
DECISION: option 3 (skill-level enforcement) + thin slice of option 2 (engine guard)

Rationale:
§10.5.2 capability matrix is a contract with the user ("Reviewer (local) — talk only, no source edits"). A user who reads that row and notices an L1 actually got applied has been lied to — trust-eroding even on day 1. Option 1 (accept) fails on its own terms.

Option 2 alone (PATH scrub) is defense-in-depth without the contract. Brittle (engine binary lives in this app's tree, awkward to strip), produces lousy error messages ("pdflatex: command not found"), and the rig can still spawn workers with full env.

Option 3 puts the gate at the level where the capability check belongs: the rig-side processor skill knows its own rig identity (from gt context or the submit file's destination_rig). One conditional at the top of /review-pdf process. The §10.5.2 row already specifies the no-mutate branch behavior; we're just routing into a path the spec already defines.

Implementation:
- /review-pdf process (post-split: rev-per child B): on entry, probe own rig identity AND read submit-file's destination_rig. If either says reviewer-local OR identity starts with "reviewer/", take the no-mutate branch — L1/L2 → needs-followup with the §10.5.2 agent_note, L3 → full conversational treatment, no apply/build/revert/commit/version-bump.
- Engine atomics (apply, build, revert): cheap one-line $GT_RIG guard. Refuse with clear message ("apply refused: invoked under Reviewer rig identity; this rig has no source-mutation capability per spec §10.5.2"). Belt-and-braces second layer.
- Reviewer-rig pty spawn (rev-1md.2, §9.2.9): no PATH scrubbing required. Skip.

Out of scope:
- Sandboxing the pty (no chroot, seccomp, FS restrictions). User can still cd + vim manually inside the pty — that's not the threat model. We enforce the automated-pipeline boundary, not user determination.
- Blocking ad-hoc claude conversations from editing files if the user explicitly asks. Not within scope of the §10.5.3 contract.

Failure mode:
A user running /review-pdf process from a Reviewer pty hits the skill check → no-mutate branch with clear message. A user invoking the raw engine binary hits the per-subcommand $GT_RIG guard → clear refusal. A user unsetting $GT_RIG has deliberately disabled the gate (user-as-attacker; out of scope). No silent-corruption path exists.

Follow-ups: none required. Folds into rev-per's Child B + engine subcommand work.

Spec patch needed: yes — §10.5.3 final paragraph (see separate commit).
close_reason: Decision recorded as note: option 3 (skill-level enforcement) + thin slice of option 2 (engine guard). Spec patch landed in §10.5.3. Implementation work folds into rev-ek3 (/review-pdf process).

title: engine: remove legacy 4-phase orchestration subcommands (post-Electron-Submit)
description: Once /review-pdf process is the sole driver of the round-based flow (M7), the 4-phase orchestration commands have no caller. Remove or mark deprecated.
notes:
Browser viewer (review-pdf serve) is dead per M7 design decision — Electron app fully replaces it.

Candidates for removal:
- review-pdf serve (browser viewer)
- review-pdf commit-phase (phase boundary commits)
- review-pdf wait-event (browser-viewer event stream)
- review-pdf append-chat (Phase 2b surface chat log)
- review-pdf set-chat (chat state)
- review-pdf set-state (phase-machine state setter)

Candidates to KEEP (atomic operations, called by /review-pdf process):
- apply, revert, preview, build, status, extract, manual-mapping (Phase 0 still relevant if a project hasn't been extracted yet)

Also needs:
- Rewrite ~/.claude/skills/review-pdf-to-latex/SKILL.md to describe the round-based /review-pdf process workflow instead of the 4-phase one
- Audit any docs/scripts referencing the removed subcommands
- Check tests/ for tests covering removed paths
- Bump engine version (breaking change)

Should NOT block on this. Ship /review-pdf process additive first (M7); do this cleanup after.

title: desktop: M7 pick toolbar icons for Claude pane (Create Context / Sling / Fresh Start)
description: Right-drawer toolbar above the conversational pty needs three icon buttons (see milestone-7 spec patch in flight). Pick emoji for each from the candidate sets below — these are placeholders for v1, will swap to custom SVG later.
notes:
Candidate emoji to pick from:

**Create Context** (spawn new agent with bundled context)
🆕  ✨  🧠  🌱  🎯  📋  🪄  🌟  ➕  💡

**Sling** (send to another rig/crew/mayor via gt-mail)
🪃  📤  📨  🛰️  ✉️  🚀  ⚡  🔗  📡  🎯

**Fresh Start** (reset this session with handoff)
🔄  🌀  🧹  ♻️  🌅  🆑  🔃  🎬  🚿  🔁

Pick one per button; settle by eye in the running app, not in a doc. Acceptable to swap if a custom SVG arrives.

Side concerns:
- All three should render at the same visual weight (some emoji are visually heavier than others — e.g., 🪃 reads larger than ✉️ at the same font-size)
- Dark mode legibility — check ones with thin lines (♻️, 🔃) don't disappear
- Hover tooltip text per button (separate copy, file as needed)
close_reason: Pick: Create Context = ✨ (sparkle reads as 'something new appears'); Sling = 🪃 (boomerang is the gt mental model — sling and it comes back); Fresh Start = 🌅 (sunrise reads positively, not 🔄 which suggests retry-on-error). All three legible in dark mode; visual weight comparable at 16-18px font-size. Hover copy: 'Spawn worker with current context' / 'Sling to another rig or crew' / 'Reset conversation — handoff + restart'. Swap to custom SVG later; emojis are v1.

title: desktop: M6 add MD viewer (later Word) so non-PDF tree entries are openable
description: M6 left drawer dims .md and .docx in the tree (kind classifier already tags them); they're inert to click. Add MD viewer + (later) Word viewer per spec §6/§7 so the tree's full surface becomes openable.

title: desktop: M6 tree has no live filesystem watching
description: M6 tree shows whatever fs:listDir returned at expand time. Files added/removed while the app is open don't appear/disappear. v1 has no live filesystem watching; add a 'refresh' affordance (button in tree header, or context-menu 'reload folder' on a directory row) or wire fs.watch with debounced re-list.

title: desktop: M6 expanded_dirs leak stale paths across root changes
description: FileTree.expanded survives setRoot — paths from a prior root are kept in the in-memory Set + persisted to state.json. They're inert (no DOM row to attach to) but accumulate in expanded_dirs over time. Fix: scope expanded by root in state.json, or prune expanded entries that don't share the current root prefix when setRoot is called.
close_reason: Closed

title: desktop: CLI shim post-packaging distribution path
description: CLI shim currently shells into 'npm run dev' for unpackaged use. Post-packaging: bundle the binary, add a postinstall step that symlinks desktop/bin/review-pdf-app into a $PATH dir (or document manual install). Real Distribution-time path is set via REVIEW_PDF_APP_BIN env var that the shim already respects — wire the packaging step to set this and ship a symlinkable target.

title: desktop: backward-drag selection extends through sub-bullets on bulleted PDFs (PDF.js #17191)
description:
On bulleted PDFs (specifically Word-exported ones), dragging backward from the end of a bullet line picks up every span between the new endpoint and the fixed anchor in DOM order — including sub-bullets that are visually far from the cursor path. The §5.2 highlight overlay paints rects across all affected lines, making the mistake very visible.

Root cause: PDF.js's `streamTextContent` emits spans in PDF stream order, which for Word-exported bulleted PDFs does NOT match visual reading order. Mozilla's reference viewer exhibits the same behavior — see https://github.com/mozilla/pdf.js/issues/17191 (open and unresolved). Our setup is at feature parity with the reference viewer; the remaining glitch is below us in the stack.

Possible mitigations (deferred):
1. Use the structTree's `aria-owns` linkages to detect when a captured selection crosses too many structural groups (paragraph / list item) and trim or warn.
2. Lighten the persistent overlay's opacity / drop `mix-blend-mode: multiply` so noisy selections render less aggressively.

Carried from milestone #3 selection-bug-research handoff via docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #9. Repro PDF: ~/Downloads/2026 Management fellow Job Description.pdf.

title: desktop: better persistence-error UI than 2s meta-line flash
description:
`writeDrafts` failures currently flash a 2s message in the anchor meta and then disappear — easy to miss. In-memory state stays authoritative until the next successful write, so no data is lost mid-session, but the user has no way to know they should stop trusting that their work is saved.

Fix shape: persistent badge near `#pdfTitle` that says 'Drafts not saving — last error: …', clears on the next successful write. Maybe also retry the write with backoff before surfacing.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #7.

title: desktop: add multi-page fixture; verify revealAnchor cross-page path
description:
The M4 verification used `tests/fixtures/sample-annotated.pdf` which is single-page. `PdfViewer.revealAnchor` calls `gotoPage(page)` first — on a single-page doc that's a no-op, so the cross-page navigation path wasn't exercised in verification. The coordinate transform did fire and produced a correctly-positioned highlight; the page-switching code is a one-line delegate to existing `gotoPage`. Low risk in theory but the first real bug here will be in someone's multi-page review session.

Fix: add a 3+ page fixture to `tests/fixtures/` (or use one of the existing real PDFs in /Users/anthonybyrnes/Downloads/), submit a comment on a non-first page, navigate elsewhere, click the card, confirm gotoPage + highlight repaint work together.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #6.

title: desktop: standalone (no-anchor) comments + click-to-anchor affordance
description:
Spec §5.1 wants a click-to-anchor affordance + ability to leave general document-level comments. Today the bottom input flashes a 2s 'Select text in the PDF first to anchor this comment' on submit without a selection — the standalone path is explicitly out of scope per milestone #3.

Two pieces:
1. Click in the PDF to place a caret-anchor (point comment).
2. Document-level comment with no anchor at all (`anchor: null` in §8 payload?). Spec doesn't pin the data shape — needs a §8 amendment.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #5.

title: desktop: edit / delete affordances on comment cards
description:
Spec §9.1 marks edit/delete as 'Maybe' for the cards milestone, so they're not strictly M4 work, but they're the most-obvious missing card actions. The persistence layer is ready: `renderAllCards` rebuilds from `docState.comments`, so mutating that array + scheduling a write is the whole flow.

Open questions: hover-reveal vs always-visible icons; inline edit vs modal; soft-delete (status='deleted', kept in file) vs hard-delete.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #4.
notes: Each card has hover-revealed ✎/× actions in the head. Delete: window.confirm with body preview, removes from docState.comments, schedules write. Edit: loads body into bottom input, sets editingCommentId at module scope, anchor meta switches to 'Editing — Enter to save · Esc to cancel'. handleSubmit updates the existing comment in-place; Esc cancels. Doc switch clears edit/focus state. Verified by /tmp/verify-m5.js.

title: desktop: keyboard navigation in comment stream (j/k/Enter)
description:
Spec §15 wants `j` / `k` to move between cards in the right drawer and `Enter` to jump to the focused card's anchor (same as clicking it). Currently click-only — there's no focus state on the cards either.

Fix: card focus state (visual + DOM), j/k handlers gated on 'right-drawer comment stream focused' per spec, Enter triggers the same revealAnchor as click.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #3.
notes: Cards are tabindex=0 + role=button. Stream-level keydown handler: j/↓ = next, k/↑ = prev (no wrap, clamp at edges), Enter on focused card = revealAnchor. Focus tracked at module scope via focusedCommentId so renderAllCards restores focus after rebuild. Spec §15 focus discipline enforced structurally (listener scoped to #commentStream).

title: desktop: group §9.1 comment cards by engagement level
description:
Spec §9.1 says 'Group by engagement level (L1/L2/L3).' Today the cards in the right drawer are chronological (newest first) with color-coded left borders. Color makes it obvious which is which, but you can't scan 'all my redrafts' without your eye doing the filtering.

Fix: bucket `docState.comments` by `engagement_level` in renderAllCards and render three labeled sections (Comment / Redraft / Surface). Chronological order within each bucket.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-20-milestone-4-done-milestone-5-handoff.md limitation #2.
notes: renderAllCards now buckets by engagement_level into L1/L2/L3 sections (LEVEL_ORDER). Empty buckets omitted. Section header colors match card left-border accent. Chronological newest-first preserved within each bucket.

title: extract: post-run summary counts don't match resulting state.json
description:
After `review-pdf extract` against the COTA RESTORED PDF, the post-run summary line reports:

  'extracted 40 annotation(s); 1 needs_review, 8 surfaced_pending'

But `review-pdf status` against the same .review-state/ reports:

  'Counts: 40 total · 39 pending · 1 surfaced_pending'

8 surfaced_pending in summary vs 1 in actual state. The extract summary line appears to count trigger_match=True as the surfaced_pending prediction — which is what bootstrap_state SHOULD route per the rev-mvd intent — but rev-mvd only auto-routes empty-text NON-trigger_match annotations. trigger_match items stay pending and only get promoted on a subsequent `bulk-surface` call.

Fix: either bootstrap_state should auto-route trigger_match → surfaced_pending too (matching the summary line's reporting), OR the summary line should count actual post-write status from state.json (matching status output). The intent is ambiguous — pick one and align.

Recommend: bootstrap_state routes trigger_match → surfaced_pending (the workflow that surface-first ordering wants anyway), eliminating the need for an explicit bulk-surface in the common case.

Reported by python419/anthony, msg hq-wisp-pp0lbh.

title: ux/skill: --project-dir wording + venv guidance + PDF error fallback hint
description:
From rev-hgj (first-run feedback). Three updates needed in ~/.claude/skills/review-pdf-to-latex/SKILL.md:

1. Move `--project-dir` before the subcommand in every example (paired with rev-16m fix).
2. Add a 'If extract raises an extraction error, ask the user whether a recovered/clean PDF is available — corrupted streams from Acrobat edits are common.' line.
3. Add venv guidance: modern macOS / Homebrew Python is externally-managed (PEP 668); bare `pip install -e` fails without venv. Suggest `python -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install -e .`.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-handoff.md (friction list).
NOTE: Lives in the skill repo, not in review-pdf-to-latex. Split out from rev-hgj.

Skill file is not under version control; edits stand on local disk.

description:
attached_molecule: rev-wisp-qku
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T21:01:45Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor

Bundle of smaller UX wishes from first-real-run feedback. Each can be split into its own bead if/when picked up:

- **Skill wording on `--project-dir`** — every example needs the flag moved before the subcommand (paired with rev-16m fix).
- **Skill's PDF assumption** — add 'If extract raises an extraction error, ask the user whether a recovered/clean PDF is available — corrupted streams from Acrobat edits are common.'
- **Skill missing venv guidance** — modern macOS / Homebrew Python is externally-managed (PEP 668); bare `pip install -e` fails without venv. Suggest venv create + `.venv/bin/pip install -e .`.

- **No `--quiet` / `--json` flag for extract** — doubled 'Missing text' warnings drowned out important info.
- **No CLI command to mark `needs_review` as `deferred`** — e.g., 'this annotation was already applied in commit X, just defer it'. Want `review-pdf set-status --annotation-id ann-X --status deferred --reason ...` to work before mapping is resolved, OR `override-mapping --status deferred` shortcut. Status-transition table (spec §10.3) may currently disallow `needs_review → deferred`.
- **Skill should describe `--mapping-mode` UI shape** — list view? flow with candidates? (Couldn't tell from first run since viewer was broken.)

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md ('What I wished was different')
close_reason: Landed on origin/main via Refinery merge (commit 62809bb). Polecat fury's work recovered from worktree and pushed; --quiet/--json on extract + 4-line status reformat. rev-ee2 filed as follow-up.

title: extract: pdfannots invoked twice, duplicating 'Missing text' warnings
description:
attached_molecule: [deleted:rev-wisp-b35]
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-05-17T20:58:58Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main"]
dispatched_by: mayor
formula_vars: base_branch=main

Each pdfannots 'Missing text' warning prints twice during `extract`, suggesting pdfannots runs twice (likely once for annotation extraction and once for page geometry / coordinate normalization).

Cost: ~1.5s of the 3.8s extract on a 40-annotation, 10-page PDF. Would scale worse on 100+ page reviews.

Fix: cache the pdfannots result on the first call and reuse, or refactor to a single pass. Confirm with `grep 'pdfannots\.' src/review_pdf_to_latex/`.

Source: docs/handoffs/2026-05-17-first-run-cota-feedback.md (BUG #3)
close_reason: Attached dedupe filter to 'pdfannots' logger for read_annotations lifetime. pdfannots' Annotation.resolve() calls gettext() during process_file (to dedupe Skim-style contents) and our loop calls it again; both fire 'Missing text for ...' warnings for the same annotation. Filter drops exact duplicates, removed in finally. Two tests added (filter behavior + leak guard). Full suite 387 passed.

title: mol-polecat-work
description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (formula checklist + issue)
2. Work through formula steps in order (shown inline at prime time)
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the workflow template. Steps are shown inline
when you run `gt prime` — there are no separate step beads to close. Work through
the checklist, then run `gt done`.

**Speed principle:** You run the full gate suite AFTER rebasing onto the target
branch (pre-verify step). This enables the refinery to fast-path merge your MR
in ~5 seconds instead of re-running gates. If pre-verification is skipped or
stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Fix pre-existing failures on main (Refinery owns main health)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command. Empty = skip. Rig must configure for its language. |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Build fails | Fix it. Do not proceed if it won't compile. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
